scenario.description: Isabelle's snack — tiny vegan + gluten-free snack ~190 cal

recipes.label: Cinnamon & Sugar Roasted Chickpeas
ingredients: 1 15-ounce can of chickpeas or garbanzo beans
1 1/2 Tbs pure cane sugar (or any other sugar), add more if necessary
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Preheat oven to 425 F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or a silicone mat.

Step 2: Drain and rinse the chickpeas.

Step 3: Place the chickpeas between two sheets of paper towels and rub them gently to absorb the excess water. Pick out any loosened skin from the chickpeas. Dont worry if you cant get all of them out.

Step 4: Pour the dried chickpeas into a small bowl and mix in the olive oil, maple syrup, and cinnamon until well incorporated.

Step 5: Place the chickpeas on the baking sheet and bake for 15 minutes.

Step 6: After 15 minutes, give the chickpeas a quick stir. Continue baking the chickpeas at 3-4 minute increments. When they are browned and look quite dried, take them out of the oven. The longer you leave them in the oven, the crunchier they will be, but be careful not to burn the chickpeas!

Step 7: Let them cool for a few minutes and sprinkle the sugar on the roasted chickpeas. Enjoy!

recipes.label: Channa-Chickpea, Potato & Cauliflower Curry
ingredients: 1 teaspoon cumin seeds- toasted and crushed
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: In a large pot heat oil.

Step 2: Add onions saute until very soft.

Step 3: Add garlic and ginger, saute for 3-4 minutes.

Step 4: Add spices, saute with potatoes until they are covered with the spice mixture.

Step 5: Add water to cover.

Step 6: Simmer until potatoes are soft.

Step 7: Add chickpeas, and cauliflower.

Step 8: Simmer with lid on for 20 minutes,stirring occasionally.

Step 9: Season with salt,sugar and vinegar.

Step 10: Serve with brown rice

recipes.label: Chickpea and Pumpkin Curry
ingredients: 140ml coconut milk (powdered coconut milk is really useful and is what I used)
1 kg pumpkin cut into small chunks
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Heat 1 tbsp oil and cook the onions until browned.

Step 2: Add the curry paste (prepared with 1 tbsp oil, 1 tsp curry powder, 1 4 cm piece fresh ginger, 2 tbsp ground coriander, 4 cloves garlic, 1 chilli, and 1 tsp turmeric) and lightly fry for a minute or two.

Step 3: Add the pumpkin and carrot chunks and coat in the paste.Cover with the water, add bay leaves, and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes.Stir in the chickpeas and coconut milk.Cook for a further 10 minutes on a low heat or until the pumpkin in soft.Make sure to pull the bay leaves out.
